prompt: The immutable class is not truly immutable. What is the bug?
choices: The constructor stores a reference to the passed array (not a copy), and getData() returns the internal array reference; callers can mutate the array externally
int[] fields must be declared transient in immutable classes
The class should implement Cloneable to be truly immutable
explanation: Two leaks break immutability: (1) the constructor stores the caller's array reference — the caller can mutate it afterward; (2) getData() returns the internal reference — the caller receives direct access. Fix: defensive copy in constructor: this.data = d.clone(); and in accessor: return data.clone();
